Interstate Moving

A Long-Distance Move Handled With Care

Moving across state lines is a big step. New home, new city, new beginning. And somewhere in the middle of it all — your furniture, boxes, and everything that matters to you.

We help families and individuals move from New York, Westchester County, New Jersey, Connecticut, and Long Island to destinations across the country.

Our job is simple: get your things there safely, on time, and without unnecessary surprises.

Our Interstate Moving Process

Smooth From Start to Finish

01
1. Plan

We go over your details, timeline, inventory, and everything that needs to be moved.

02
2. Pack & Protect

We pack, wrap, and protect your items for the road ahead.

03
3. Transport

Your belongings are carefully loaded and transported by experienced movers.

04
4. Deliver

We deliver to your new home and place everything where you want it.

FAQ

Interstate Moving Questions

Do you provide interstate moving from New York?

Yes. Atlantic Star Relocations handles interstate moving from NYC, Westchester County, New Jersey, Connecticut, Long Island and nearby areas to destinations across the United States.

What is the difference between interstate moving and long-distance moving?

Interstate moving means your move crosses state lines. Long-distance moving usually refers to a move over a longer mileage range, even if it stays within one state. Many out-of-state moves are both interstate and long-distance moves.

Can you help with packing for an out-of-state move?

Yes. We can help with full packing, partial packing, fragile items, kitchen packing, wardrobe boxes, furniture wrapping and preparation for long-distance transportation.

How are furniture and fragile items protected during interstate moving?

Furniture is wrapped, padded and secured for transport. Fragile items are packed carefully, labeled, and loaded with attention to placement so they are better protected during the interstate move.

Do you offer interstate moving from Westchester County?

Yes. We provide interstate moving services from Westchester County cities including Yonkers, New Rochelle, White Plains, Larchmont, Scarsdale, Port Chester and surrounding areas.

Do you handle interstate moves from NYC apartments?

Yes. We help with interstate apartment moves from Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx and Staten Island, including elevator scheduling, building access planning, packing and delivery coordination.

Can you move me from New York to Florida?

Yes. New York to Florida moving is a common interstate route. We can help with planning, packing, pickup, transportation and delivery for homes, apartments and smaller moves.

Can you move me from New York to New Jersey or Connecticut?

Yes. Even shorter moves across state lines can be interstate moves. We handle moves between NY, NJ and CT with the same attention to scheduling, protection and delivery details.

How do I get an interstate moving quote?

Send us your pickup address, delivery address, move date, inventory list, packing needs and any stairs, elevators or building rules. The more details we have, the more accurate your interstate moving quote will be.

What affects the cost of an interstate move?

Pricing depends on distance, move size, inventory, packing, access at pickup and delivery, stairs, elevators, special items, timing and whether storage or additional services are needed.

Do you offer door-to-door interstate moving?

Yes. We can provide door-to-door interstate moving with pickup, loading, transportation, delivery and placement of your items at the new home.

Can you move only a few items to another state?

Yes. We can help with small interstate moves, partial loads, furniture-only moves and selected item deliveries depending on the route, timing and availability.
